query:

state.history_summary:

user: The user confirms the name change.

assistant: The assistant confirms the name change was successful.

candidate_answers.text: You’re welcome. The passenger name update is already complete, so there’s nothing else you need to do.
The visible state shows the requested passenger update has already been applied and the user is simply ending the conversation.

provenance.source_type: manual annotation from real tau2 episode
provenance.decision_rule: Use the later closing user turn because it is an exact source user message after the passenger name update has already been completed, so stop is the only grounded next action.
provenance.scoring_method: Manual one-step proxy scoring from the decision-point state. Scores reflect which action most directly advances the user's request using only visible conversation evidence.
notes: query comes directly from the selected user message including the stop marker
history_summary stops at the completed name-change confirmation and excludes future content
candidate_answers are manual non-leaky drafts grounded only in visible context
special case: terminal thank-you turn after successful passenger update
